reasoning: At 4x the potion has genuine charm — round pink bottle with polka dots, a cork stopper, and leafy vines give it a friendly sticker-book personality. The strawberry-like spots add whimsy. Silhouette at 4x on dark bg is readable once you know what it is, but the sprawling vine tendrils muddy the outline: the bottle shape is not instantly readable as a potion without the context of the spots. At 1x (leftmost panel) it collapses to a small pink blob with green noise — the distinctive bottle silhouette is lost. On the light bg the green vines blend somewhat into the warm-toned background, reducing edge pop. Charm score is high (round, soft, personality-filled) but silhouette clarity at 1x and the vine tangle prevent a clean pass.
